sn36.22:1.1: “Monks, I will teach you a one-hundred-and-eight exposition that is a Dhamma exposition.
sn36.22:1.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.” “As you say, lord,” the monks responded to him. The Blessed One said:
sn36.22:1.3: “And which one-hundred-and-eight exposition is a Dhamma exposition?
sn36.22:1.4: There is the exposition whereby I have spoken of two feelings,
sn36.22:1.5: the exposition whereby I have spoken of three feelings…
sn36.22:2.5: A feeling of pleasure, a feeling of pain, a feeling of neither pleasure nor pain.
sn36.22:2.8: The pleasure-faculty, the pain-faculty, the happiness-faculty, the distress-faculty, the equanimity-faculty.
sn36.22:2.11: A feeling born of eye-contact, a feeling born of ear-contact… nose-contact… tongue-contact… body-contact…
sn36.22:2.15: Six happiness-explorations, six distress-explorations, six equanimity-explorations.
sn36.22:2.18: Six kinds of house-based happiness & six kinds of renunciation-based happiness; six kinds of house-based distress & six kinds of renunciation-based distress; six kinds of house-based equanimity & six kinds of renunciation-based equanimity.
sn36.22:2.20: “And which are the one hundred and eight feelings?
sn36.22:2.21: Thirty-six past feelings, thirty-six future feelings, and thirty-six present feelings.
sn36.22:2.22: These are the one hundred and eight feelings.
sn36.22:2.23: “And this, monks, is the one-hundred-and-eight exposition that is a Dhamma exposition.”
